violations.description: Class is named `SmokeTests` and lives in the `Admin.UI.E2E` folder/namespace, but it is HTTP-only inspection via `WebApplicationFactory<Program>` — no browser is launched, no JS runs, no Blazor Server hydration is exercised. Reviewers and contributors will mistake these for browser E2E coverage. The file shares its purpose with `BrowserSmokeTests.cs` (the real browser smoke pass), causing additional confusion. Note also the file is missing `[Trait("Category", "E2E")]` that every other class in the folder declares.
violations.suggestion: Rename the class (and file) to `LandingPageMarkupSmokeTests` or `LandingPageHttpSmokeTests`, or move it to a non-E2E test project (e.g., a `Hexalith.EventStore.Admin.UI.Tests` markup smoke project). Add `[Trait("Category", "E2E")]` only if it stays in this project.

violations.description: `AdminUIE2EFixture` is defined but never used as a fixture by any test class in the repository. A repo-wide grep finds it only in the file itself and in BMad documentation artifacts; no test class declares `IClassFixture<AdminUIE2EFixture>` or `ICollectionFixture<AdminUIE2EFixture>`. The remarks comment even claims it is the playwright install entry point, which is misleading — `PlaywrightFixture` is the actual fixture in use.
violations.suggestion: Delete `AdminUIE2EFixture.cs`. The browser-aware `PlaywrightFixture` already covers the E2E fixture concern. If a `WebApplicationFactory`-based fixture is genuinely needed for the `SmokeTests` HTTP-only class, refactor `SmokeTests` to consume it explicitly — but right now it uses `IClassFixture<WebApplicationFactory<Program>>` directly.

violations.description: The `(IBrowserContext context, IPage page) = await _fixture.CreatePageAsync(); await using (context) { ... }` boilerplate is repeated 12 times across 4 test files (5x in BrowserSmokeTests, 3x in Dw5SidebarShortcutBrowserAtddTests, 2x each in Dw5DialogAccessibilityBrowserAtddTests and Dw5TypeCatalogNavigationBrowserAtddTests). Every existing test opens this boilerplate, and every new test will have to repeat it. The pattern obscures the test intent and ensures one type of bug (forgetting `await using`) cannot be fixed in one place.
violations.suggestion: Add `PlaywrightFixture.RunWithPageAsync(Func<IPage, Task> body)` or `Func<IPage, Task<T>>` that owns the context lifecycle internally. Each test body becomes `await _fixture.RunWithPageAsync(async page => { ... });`, removing the tuple destructure and the `await using` block from every test.

violations.description: CSS/ARIA selectors are duplicated as inline string literals across multiple test files: `main[role='main']` appears 5 times across BrowserSmokeTests and Dw5DialogAccessibilityBrowserAtddTests; `.admin-sidebar nav[aria-label='Main navigation']` appears 9 times across BrowserSmokeTests, Dw5SidebarShortcutBrowserAtddTests, and Dw5TypeCatalogNavigationBrowserAtddTests; `h1:has-text('Type Catalog')` appears 3 times in Dw5TypeCatalogNavigationBrowserAtddTests. A UI selector change (e.g., renaming `.admin-sidebar` or removing the `aria-label`) forces a manual sweep with no compiler help.
violations.suggestion: Extract a `Selectors` static class (or `internal static class AdminUiSelectors`) with `private const string` entries (`MainContent = "main[role='main']"`, `SidebarNav = ".admin-sidebar nav[aria-label='Main navigation']"`, `TypeCatalogHeading = "h1:has-text('Type Catalog')"`, etc.). Reference these from every test.
violations.code_snippet:
await page.WaitForSelectorAsync("main[role='main']");
// ...repeated 5 times across BrowserSmokeTests + Dw5Dialog... tests

violations.description: `NavigateToCommandSandbox` and `NavigateToEventDebugger` are private helpers whose only callers are inside `[Fact(Skip = ...)]` tests, and both helpers begin by performing setup steps (`GotoAsync("/streams")`, `WaitForSelectorAsync(...)`) before unconditionally throwing `InvalidOperationException`. The setup work is unreachable from any callable test, the helpers cannot be called from anywhere else (no `internal` exposure), and the throw-after-setup pattern guarantees that even if Skip is removed, the test will fail at the throw line rather than make progress. The result is helpers that look like real navigation but execute zero meaningful steps.
violations.suggestion: Replace each helper body with a single `throw new NotImplementedException(...)` (no preceding work) and add an XML doc comment stating that the body is a placeholder pending dev seeding. Better still: rename to `SeedAndOpenCommandSandboxDialogAsync` so the name advertises that seeding is the unfinished part, not navigation.

violations.description: `AssertSidebarNavFromTypeCatalog` contains two `Shouldly` assertions and a `WaitForAsync` that would throw `TimeoutException` (an implicit assertion) buried inside the helper. Three of the four `[Fact]` methods delegate their entire body to this helper, so when one fails the xUnit failure message points at `AssertSidebarNavFromTypeCatalog` instead of the specific test (`SidebarNav_From_TypesTabCommands_TransitionsUrlAndVisiblePage`). The helper name does carry `Assert`, which the rubric explicitly flags as a hidden-assertion pattern.
violations.suggestion: Either inline the loop into each test (preferred for E2E where failure attribution matters) or pass a `customMessage` to each Shouldly call that names the calling test (e.g., include `startingUrl` in the failure message — already done for URL but not for the H1 detach wait). At minimum, rename the helper to `NavigateSidebarFromTypeCatalogAsync` (action verb, not assertion verb) so its role is unambiguous.

violations.description: `_dialogTimeout` (5s) in `Dw5DialogAccessibilityBrowserAtddTests`, `_navTimeout` (3s) in `Dw5TypeCatalogNavigationBrowserAtddTests`, `_shortcutTimeout` (3s) in `Dw5SidebarShortcutBrowserAtddTests` each define their own per-file `TimeSpan` constant. The 3-second value is duplicated and the 2/3/5-second values are scattered, with no central catalog. The `BrowserSmokeTests` budget comment in `Dw5TypeCatalogNavigationBrowserAtddTests` line 19 even references `BrowserSmokeTests` informally — but `BrowserSmokeTests.Dashboard_ShellRendersWithin3Seconds` hard-codes the literal `3000` (line 87), not a shared constant. Updating the budget requires touching multiple files.
violations.suggestion: Add `internal static class E2eTimeouts { public static readonly TimeSpan ShellHydration = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(3); public static readonly TimeSpan SidebarNavigation = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(3); public static readonly TimeSpan DialogOpen = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5); public static readonly TimeSpan ShortcutResponse = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(3); }`. Reference from every test file.

violations.description: `sw.ElapsedMilliseconds.ShouldBeLessThan(3000)` and `sw.ElapsedMilliseconds.ShouldBeLessThan(2000)` (SmokeTests line 75) are bare integer literals with no named constant. `SmokeTests.cs` also references `2000` in a comment as `AC 14: shell renders within 2 seconds` — the AC number is captured in a comment, not in a named member, so the constant cannot be referenced elsewhere when the AC budget changes.
violations.suggestion: Replace `3000`/`2000` with `(int)E2eTimeouts.ShellHydration.TotalMilliseconds` (from the proposed shared catalog) or define `private const int ShellHydrationBudgetMs = 3000;` per file with the AC reference in XML doc.

violations.description: `SmokeTests` is the only test class in the `Admin.UI.E2E` test project that does not carry `[Trait("Category", "E2E")]`. BrowserSmokeTests, Dw5DialogAccessibilityBrowserAtddTests, Dw5TypeCatalogNavigationBrowserAtddTests, and Dw5SidebarShortcutBrowserAtddTests all declare it. Result: trait-filter test runs (`--filter Category=E2E`) silently skip the SmokeTests file, so the filtered runs reported in CI may be misleadingly green.
violations.suggestion: Add `[Trait("Category", "E2E")]` to the `SmokeTests` class declaration. If the intent is that this file is NOT E2E (which the contents support — it is HTTP-only), then move the file to a non-E2E test project per the renaming violation above.
violations.code_snippet:
// no [Trait(...)] attribute on:
public class SmokeTests : IClassFixture<WebApplicationFactory<Program>>

violations.description: Three out of four tests share an identical Arrange-and-fetch sequence: `HttpClient client = _factory.CreateClient(); string content = await (await client.GetAsync("/")).Content.ReadAsStringAsync();`. The fourth (`ShellRendersWithin2Seconds`) is a near-duplicate that adds a Stopwatch. The result string is then asserted against multiple `ShouldContain` calls per test — but the multi-assertion split across tests means one failed fetch fails three tests separately with three near-identical stack frames.
violations.suggestion: Either consolidate the markup asserts into one `LandingPage_HasExpectedAccessibilityMarkup` test with grouped Shouldly assertions (`content.ShouldSatisfyAllConditions(...)`), or extract `private async Task<string> GetLandingPageHtmlAsync()` and reuse. The latter preserves test granularity while removing the copy-paste.

violations.description: The composite locator `"fluent-text-field:has(input[placeholder='Search commands, pages, actions...']), input[placeholder='Search commands, pages, actions...']"` is a complex multi-selector built inline. Although it appears only once in this file, the inner placeholder string `'Search commands, pages, actions...'` is duplicated within the same selector and is the kind of UX-copy detail that breaks if the placeholder is ever localized or edited. Pair this with `'fluent-dialog[aria-label=\"Event payload\"]'` in Dw5DialogAccessibility... and the pattern of inline composite selectors with embedded copy is established.
violations.suggestion: Extract the placeholder text and the composite selector into constants on a `Selectors` helper (`CommandPaletteSearchInput`, `CommandPalettePlaceholder`). Then a UX-copy change is a one-line edit and a single failing test, not silent staleness.

violations.description: Skip-reason constants `_ac8SkipReason` (lines 26-30) and `_ac9SkipReason` (lines 31-35) carry near-identical 4-line prose explanations that differ only in component name (CommandSandbox vs EventDebugger). The bodies are otherwise structurally identical. This is a low-risk maintenance issue but it does mean future edits (e.g., updating the evidence-index policy reference) must be made in two places.
violations.suggestion: Refactor to a parameterized helper or string.Format pattern: `private const string SkipReasonTemplate = "...{0}...{1}...";` then `private static readonly string _ac8SkipReason = string.Format(SkipReasonTemplate, "CommandSandbox", "stream/command context");`. Or accept the duplication explicitly as documentation prose.
violations.code_snippet:
private const string _ac8SkipReason = "ATDD red phase — DW5 AC#8 ...";
private const string _ac9SkipReason = "ATDD red phase — DW5 AC#9 ...";  // near-identical body

violations.description: `page.Locator(".admin-sidebar:visible").First` mixes a CSS class selector with the Playwright `:visible` pseudo and `.First` chaining. The same pattern appears at line 120. The `:visible` pseudo is Playwright-specific and not part of CSS — readers unfamiliar with Playwright may try to validate the selector against the DOM as plain CSS and find no match.
violations.suggestion: Wrap in an `ILocator GetVisibleSidebar(IPage page)` helper with an XML doc comment explaining the `:visible` pseudo, or use `page.Locator(".admin-sidebar").Locator(":visible").First` (slightly less compact, more explicit).

violations.description: `typeof(Hexalith.EventStore.Admin.UI.AdminUIServiceExtensions).Assembly.Location` uses a fully qualified type name even though the file has `using Hexalith.EventStore.Admin.UI;` at line 4. Line 51 references the same type unqualified (`typeof(AdminUIServiceExtensions).Assembly`). Inconsistent.
violations.suggestion: Drop the fully qualified prefix on line 46. Use `typeof(AdminUIServiceExtensions).Assembly.Location`.
Reformat the inline lambda bodies to Allman: opening `{` on its own line after `=>`, matching the project's convention. Or extract to a named method.

recommendations: Delete `AdminUIE2EFixture.cs` — it is dead code with no callers across the repo. `PlaywrightFixture` is the actual fixture in use; `SmokeTests` (HTTP-only) consumes `WebApplicationFactory<Program>` directly via `IClassFixture<>`.

Rename `SmokeTests` → `LandingPageMarkupSmokeTests` (or move to a non-E2E project). The current name + folder strongly implies browser E2E coverage when the contents are HTTP markup inspection — the misleading-naming risk is high because `BrowserSmokeTests.cs` already exists as the browser counterpart.

Add `PlaywrightFixture.RunWithPageAsync(Func<IPage, Task> body)` to eliminate the 12-occurrence `(IBrowserContext, IPage) = await _fixture.CreatePageAsync(); await using (context) { ... }` boilerplate across the four browser test files.

Introduce `internal static class AdminUiSelectors` (or `E2eSelectors`) holding `private const string` entries for the 9× duplicated `.admin-sidebar nav[aria-label='Main navigation']`, the 5× duplicated `main[role='main']`, the 3× duplicated `h1:has-text('Type Catalog')`, the dialog `aria-label='Event payload'` selector, and the command palette placeholder text.

Introduce `internal static class E2eTimeouts` consolidating `ShellHydration`, `SidebarNavigation`, `DialogOpen`, `ShortcutResponse` — replaces three per-file `TimeSpan` constants plus the bare `2000`/`3000` literals in BrowserSmokeTests and SmokeTests.

In `Dw5DialogAccessibilityBrowserAtddTests`, strip the unreachable `GotoAsync`/`WaitForSelectorAsync` calls from `NavigateToCommandSandbox` / `NavigateToEventDebugger` (everything before the unconditional throw is dead). Rename to `SeedAndOpenCommandSandboxDialogAsync` so the name signals what is unfinished.
Add `[Trait("Category", "E2E")]` to `SmokeTests` for consistency with the other four classes — or, preferably, relocate it as recommended above so the trait honestly reflects the contents.

Either inline `AssertSidebarNavFromTypeCatalog` into each `[Fact]` (preferred for E2E debuggability) or rename it `NavigateSidebarFromTypeCatalogAsync` to advertise that it acts rather than asserts — the current `Assert*` prefix triggers the hidden-assertions rubric.
summary: Maintainability is poor: one dead fixture, one misleadingly named class missing the E2E trait, dead branches inside skipped scaffolds, plus systemic duplication — the `CreatePageAsync()`/`await using (context)` block is copy-pasted 12 times across four files, and core selectors and timeouts are duplicated as inline literals rather than centralized constants.
